name: CEO
description: Cross-cutting decisions — management style, quality bar, verdict posture, escalation threshold

domains.name: Identity & Context
domains.hint: What software they build, who uses it, what 'production' means for this operator, and who gets hurt if something ships broken

domains.name: Management Style
domains.hint: Bias toward shipping vs. holding, tolerance for risk, preference for being interrupted vs. letting the factory decide autonomously

domains.name: Quality Bar
domains.hint: What makes a blocking vs. non-blocking issue. Tolerance for rough edges in a first release. What a blocking review failure looks like vs. a non-blocking one.

domains.name: Review & Verdict Posture
domains.hint: When to accept or override no-ship verdicts. Which failure types always block regardless of severity. Which never block regardless of how a reviewer flags them. Loop tolerance before escalation.

domains.name: Security Posture
domains.hint: Severity thresholds. What can be accepted with documented risk vs. must be fixed. How context (internal tool vs. user-facing) changes the calculus.

domains.name: Copy Voice
domains.hint: Tone and formality. Technical vs. accessible. Error message philosophy and format. Anti-patterns to avoid. Examples of copy they love and hate.

domains.name: Escalation Threshold
domains.hint: Situations that always interrupt the operator. What the factory should never decide autonomously. Tolerance for a wrong autonomous call vs. over-interrupting.
